channel.title: French Coffee on Home Espresso Lab
channel.description: Recent content in French Coffee on Home Espresso Lab

item.title: What Is French Coffee? Café au Lait, Café Crème, Noisette, Allongé & Gourmand Explained
item.description: French coffee is not one drink but a family — café au lait, café crème, café noisette, café allongé, café gourmand, and café filtre — each with its own ratio, ritual, and place in the bistro day. Learn the full French coffee tradition from 1686 Café de Procope to the modern Paris specialty scene, the chicory question, how French names map to Italian originals (noisette = macchiato, allongé = lungo), brand and bistro standards, plus how to order coffee in Paris, Marseille, or Lyon.

item.title: Café au Lait: What It Is, How to Make It & Café au Lait vs Latte
item.description: Café au lait explained — the classic French and New Orleans drip-coffee-and-hot-milk drink. Recipe, 1:1 ratio, the chicory New Orleans version, and how it differs from a latte, cappuccino, and café con leche.
